decision_reasons: The absolute historical set still contains the CY-W3 to DB-W1 consecutive weak pair.
Trailing horizons of 6 and 8 windows were inspected after outcomes were known and were not preregistered.
Selecting a favorable trailing horizon now would create post-hoc gate-fitting risk.
DI-W3 per-window gap reasons remain unavailable in committed artifacts.
Trailing observations are descriptive and cannot override the historical gate without a preregistered specification and independent evidence.

required_before_any_new_evidence: freeze the trailing horizon without using future outcomes
retain the weak threshold below 5 unless separately justified before evidence
freeze adjacency and weak-count pass criteria
define how watch windows are reported without converting them into weak windows
define minimum independent evidence required for validation
define fail-safe handling for missing row-level attribution
state that historical weakness remains visible even if a future trailing gate passes

next_safe_step: Checkpoint DX docs-only stability-gate preregistration package before any new evidence or execution approval.
